title: Simulation: Monthly Budget
perfectScoreMsg: You got {{score}} out of {{total}} scenarios correct! You're mastering the art of prioritizing needs over wants!
lessonLabel: Lesson: Always pay for needs first, then allocate remaining money to wants. This ensures financial stability!
lowScoreMsg: You got {{score}} out of {{total}} scenarios correct. Remember: Needs come first, then wants!
tipLabel: Tip: Allocate 60% to needs (essential expenses) and 40% to wants (optional expenses) for a balanced budget.

scenarios.title: Monthly Allowance Planning
scenarios.description: You get ₹2000 as monthly allowance. How should you plan it to cover essentials and still enjoy life?

options.text: Spend mostly on wants without tracking
Allocate 50% needs, 30% savings, 20% wants
Save the entire allowance, skip wants
Spend randomly without a plan

scenarios.title: Part-Time Job Earnings
scenarios.description: You earn ₹2500 from a part-time job. Which approach ensures financial stability and fun?

options.text: Spend all immediately on desires
Invest everything, ignore monthly needs
Cover essentials first, allocate for fun and save remainder
Keep cash unused without purpose

scenarios.title: Scholarship Budgeting
scenarios.description: You receive ₹1800 as scholarship. How do you make it last the month efficiently?

options.text: Cover essentials first, allocate small amount for fun
Spend all on luxury items
Ignore budgeting, spend randomly
Invest full amount in risky assets

scenarios.title: Gift Money Management
scenarios.description: You get ₹3000 as a gift. What is the smart monthly budget strategy?

options.text: Spend it all at once for instant pleasure
Hoard it without planning
Give all to friends or charity immediately
Allocate for needs, savings, and some fun

scenarios.title: Seasonal Earnings Budget
scenarios.description: You earn ₹2200 from a summer job. Which plan maximizes benefits while avoiding waste?

options.text: Spend randomly without thinking
Plan for essentials, savings, and treat yourself wisely
Use it to pay off debt only
Save entire earnings without spending
